title: Dungeon Crusade: Let's Create a Quest
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fIo8SE2ky98

Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through the early stages of designing a custom quest for Dungeon Crusade, partly because he wants to play a *condensed* two-hour version of the game and partly because nobody else seems to be making community quests for it.

The structure: a short prologue using three Avalon Adventure Board Game cards (Thunder Coast → Lair of Aben → Forgotten Ruins) to bring a two- or three-hero solo party into the main map. The quest proper drops mining, tavern tasks, blessings, and the celebration-day return-to-village loop entirely — one sitting, one descent, leveling done at an in-map village elder rather than back home. The narrative: stop **Lord Blackhand** and his generals **Razorfang** and **Nefarion** from completing a Ritual of Awakening that would spawn three level-three shadow servants on three altars. Blackhand can only be killed by the unholy-blight dagger, which only manifests once three orbs of power are placed on those same altars, and only a level-three hero can wield it. A randomised onyx key, magical red/blue/ivory key doors, and a small lockpicking mini-game gate the path inward.

He tightens the bestiary too — a single black-hand cult theme runs through the chosen minions (cultists, wraiths, skeletons) and themed champions across all three levels. His standing trap-reward house rule applies. He invites the community to do the same.

----

Daniel's first design instinct is to *cut* — no mining, no tavern, no celebration day, no return to village. Is the cleanest way to shorten a long game to remove subsystems wholesale, or to compress them?
